6.reason: [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use 'fgets' instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, 'gets' is removed entirely, leading to a compilation error.]

15.reason: [Potential out-of-bounds access: if input is "0.0", then n+2 may exceed string length, causing undefined behavior or crash. Also, no check for end of string.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Potential out-of-bounds access: if input is "0.0", then n+2 may exceed string length, causing undefined behavior or crash.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Potential out-of-bounds access: if input is "0.0", then n+2 may exceed string length, causing undefined behavior or crash. Also, no check for end of string.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Infinite loop possible if all characters after "0." are zeros or if input ends before n+2; also accesses beyond string length without bounds check.]

17.reason: [Accessing s[n+4] may go out of bounds; also, input from 'gets' does not include '\n', so this condition is incorrect.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary check: '\n' may not be in the string (e.g., if input ends with '\0' only), and n+4 can easily go out of bounds.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Accessing s[n+4] may go out of bounds; also, input from 'gets' does not include '\n', so this condition is incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary check: s[n+4] may be beyond string length; also, newline may not be present as gets() discards it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect: the input string does not necessarily contain '\n' as part of the string read by 'gets'; 'gets' discards the newline. Also, accessing s[n+4] may go out of bounds if the string is short (e.g., "0.1").]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect index: should check for end of string ('\0'), not '\n'; also n+4 may be out of bounds.]

28.reason: [This should be 'else if' to avoid reprocessing numbers starting with '0'; as written, both blocks may execute for "0.x" inputs.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This should be 'else if' to avoid processing both branches when s[0]=='0'; leads to incorrect double output for numbers starting with '0'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This should be 'else if' to avoid reprocessing numbers starting with '0'; as written, both blocks may execute for "0.x" inputs.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Should be 'else if' to avoid reprocessing non-zero numbers after zero case; leads to double output for non-zero inputs that also satisfy first condition partially.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This should be 'else if' or 'else', because the two conditions (s[0]=='0' and s[0]!='0') are mutually exclusive. Using two separate 'if's causes both blocks to potentially execute if the first block modifies s[0], but more importantly, after handling s[0]=='0', we should not re-check. However, the real issue is logic duplication and possible double-output, but the immediate compilation error is due to 'gets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Should be 'else if' to avoid reprocessing numbers starting with '0' that already entered first block.]

43.reason: [No bounds check: if there's no '.' in input (though problem says there is), this loops forever or accesses out of bounds.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [If there's no '.' in the string (though problem says there is), this loop runs forever. But more critically, after loop, n1 is index of '.', so exponent should be n1-1, but printing logic includes all digits including those after decimal without proper handling.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [No bounds check: if there's no '.' in input (though problem says there is), this loops forever or accesses out of bounds.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Infinite loop if there's no '.' in the string; no bounds check against 'l'.]

50.reason: [This prints all non-decimal-point digits after the first, which may include trailing zeros or too many digits; violates requirement that base has only one digit before decimal and rest after without trailing zeros.]
